knowledge_gaps: ROIC-WACC spread trajectory can serve as a diagnostic for distinguishing first-round from second-round commodity-inflation effects.
Second-round effects vs first-round price level shifts
The passthrough coefficient may have structurally re-risen post-COVID due to firms learning they can raise prices without losing volume.
Petrodollar recycling creates a structural self-hedging asymmetry for the US during oil shocks via GCC SWF Treasury purchases (r≈0.6 correlation with oil prices).
The dollar cycle is approaching an inflection point based on twin-deficit fundamentals (~10% GDP combined) and declining foreign UST purchases (50% to 25% of issuance).

The green energy transition will structurally change commodity-inflation transmission dynamics, shifting from oil/gas to metals (copper, lithium, rare earths) with different supply elasticities and financialization characteristics.
Food price inflation transmission in developed markets
OCF/revenue growth ratio with 1.0x threshold is the firm-level commodity passthrough diagnostic.
Dedollarization is gradually increasing commodity-inflation passthrough variance across countries.
Energy price passthrough lags by sector
focus.instruction: Build foundational understanding: define core concepts, establish theoretical frameworks, identify key empirical patterns, and map connections to related topics.
